2603.24079.title: When Understanding Becomes a Risk: Authenticity and Safety Risks in the Emerging Image Generation Paradigm
2603.24079.paper_summary: Recently, multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have emerged as a unified paradigm for language and image generation. Compared with diffusion models, MLLMs possess a much stronger capability for semantic understanding, enabling them to process more complex textual inputs and comprehend richer contextual meanings. However, this enhanced semantic ability may also introduce new and potentially greater safety risks. Taking diffusion models as a reference point, we systematically analyze and compare the safety risks of emerging MLLMs along two dimensions: unsafe content generation and fake image synthesis. Across multiple unsafe generation benchmark datasets, we observe that MLLMs tend to generate more unsafe images than diffusion models. This difference partly arises because diffusion models often fail to interpret abstract prompts, producing corrupted outputs, whereas MLLMs can comprehend these prompts and generate unsafe content. For current advanced fake image detectors, MLLM-generated images are also notably harder to identify. Even when detectors are retrained with MLLMs-specific data, they can still be bypassed by simply providing MLLMs with longer and more descriptive inputs. Our measurements indicate that the emerging safety risks of the cutting-edge generative paradigm, MLLMs, have not been sufficiently recognized, posing new challenges to real-world safety.
2603.24079.paper_summary_zh: 最近，多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）作為語言和圖像生成的統一範式出現。<lb/>與擴散模型相比，MLLMs 具有更強的語義理解能力，使它們能夠處理更複雜的文本輸入並理解更豐富的上下文意義。<lb/>然而，這種增強的語義能力也可能引入新的且潛在更大的安全風險。<lb/>以擴散模型為參考點，我們系統地分析和比較了新興 MLLMs 在兩個維度上的安全風險：不安全內容生成和假圖像合成。<lb/>在多個不安全生成基準數據集上，我們觀察到 MLLMs 傾向於生成比擴散模型更多的不安全圖像。<lb/>這種差異部分源於擴散模型通常無法解釋抽象提示，導致產生損壞的輸出，而 MLLMs 能夠理解這些提示並生成不安全內容。<lb/>對於當前先進的假圖像檢測器，MLLM 生成的圖像也顯著更難識別。<lb/>即使檢測器使用 MLLMs 特定數據重新訓練，它們仍然可以通過簡單地提供更長且更具描述性的輸入來被繞過。<lb/>我們的測量表明，前沿生成範式 MLLMs 的新興安全風險尚未得到充分認識，對現實世界的安全構成了新的挑戰。
2603.24079.author: Ye Leng et.al.
2603.24079.authors: Ye Leng, Junjie Chu, Mingjie Li, Chenhao Lin, Chao Shen, Michael Backes, Yun Shen, Yang Zhang
